purpose: Comprehensive gap analysis report (2026-03-23) comparing the new modular CLAUDE.md documentation system against the original monolithic structure. Documents the new hub-and-spoke architecture (5 hubs, 23 modules, 5 preserved instruction files = 7,726 lines total), reports CLAUDE.md size reduction (989 -> 343 lines, 65.3%), validates all 70+ cross-references resolve, lists 7 gaps (3 moderate severity for cross-module inconsistencies G-1 metrics.json, G-2 deviations severity, G-3 dashboard_protocol.md missing), confirms zero behavioral regressions across 5 rule categories, and recommends remediation.
gotchas: G-1 (Moderate): metrics.json missing from role.md allowed files table -- contradicts CLAUDE.md and compaction_recovery.md
G-2 (Moderate): severity field missing from data_architecture.md deviations schema -- contradicts deviations.md
G-3 (Moderate): dashboard_protocol.md not listed in tracker_master_instructions.md Module Index
Zero behavioral regressions across master constraints, execution rules, worker constraints, planning rules, dashboard protocol, failure handling
Restructure increases total lines (~7,726) but reduces per-agent context load via hub-and-spoke + Document Reference Map triggers
patterns: Hub-and-spoke: hubs reference modules via 'Full details:' / 'Read:' pointers
Document Reference Map in CLAUDE.md (NON-NEGOTIABLE) maps scenarios to required reads
Coverage Matrix maps original sections to new module locations
Trigger-based reading: agents only read modules when triggered by specific scenarios
conventions: Module organization: agent/master/, agent/worker/, agent/core/, agent/_commands/
Severity grades: Moderate, Low, Very Low (no High found)
Pass/Fail verdict per behavioral rule category
